source_metadata.title_raw: Angie Palmerin, Esq. - A-18-251 - December 7, 2018 - West Sacramento

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Angie Palmerin, Esq. 
3706 Abaco Road 
West Sacramento, CA 95691 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-251 
Dear Ms. Palmerin: 
December 7, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the gift provisions of 
the Act and not under other prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not 
the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
As a public official subject to the Act's gift provisions, is payment by your spouse's 
employer, Raley's, for his education, housing, and travel expenses associated with USC's Food 
Industry Management program, your habitation of a program-associated apartment for 
approximately two-and-a-half months, and your program-associated travel expenses considered 
gifts to you under the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
No. Your husband's independent relationship with Raley's removes the food retailer's 
payments under the program from the Act's gift prohibitions. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTOR 
You are a staff attorney with the Department of Forestry and Fire Protection ("Cal Fire") 
holding a position designated in the agency's conflict-of-interest code. Your duties consist mainly 
of providing legal advice as it relates to Cal Fire's recovery program, which seeks to recover 
suppression costs incurred during fire suppression activities. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-251 
Page No. 2 
Your husband has been in-house counsel for Raley's since January 2014. Raley's is a 
privately-held supermarket chain that operates stores throughout norther California and Nevada. 
Although Cal Fire may purchase goods from Raley's during fire incidents, you have never been 
involved in making decisions or providing advice regarding grocery retailers. 
Your husband will be attending a semester long Food Industry Management program at the 
University of Southern California starting on January 3, 2019. Raley's will be paying for his tuition, 
books, and school fees of approximately $28,000 and his housing of approximately $20,000 for the 
four months he will be attending USC. In addition, they offered to pay for four roundtrip flights 
between Sacramento and Los Angeles for him or a family member. Your understanding of the 
purpose of Raley's USC program is to develop the successful applicant's knowledge in retail 
management and leadership skills, so that he or she can then implement those skills at Raley's to 
benefit the company. 
·Every year, Raley's advertises for the Food Industry Management program to management 
level employees. Raley's selects one employee every year to attend the program. The chosen 
employee will have his or her education, housing, and travel expenses paid for by Raley's. 
Interested employees must submit an application and personal statement to Raley's for 
consideration. Raley's will then select some of the employees who have applied to participate in 
an initial interview. Raley's will then narrow down its selection process and invite a smaller 
group of employees to participate in a second interview with different interviewers. Once 
chosen, the recipient has to separately apply to USC by submitting a school application, 
transcripts, resume, and personal statement. 
Your husband was chosen by Raley's through this process to attend USC. USC 
subsequently accepted your husband. Your understanding of the purpose of the program is to 
develop the recipient's knowledge in retail management and leadership skills, so that the recipient 
can then implement such knowledge and skills at Raley's to benefit the company . . 
During the time your husband attends USC, you plan on visiting him for approximately two-
and-a-half months at the apartment Raley's will provide. You will not receive Cal Fire 
compensation during this time. 
The apartment property manager/landlord knows of your intention to stay with your 
husband in the apartment and requires that your name be on the lease. Raley's is aware that you 
wish to stay with your husband in the apartment, but the company's offer to pay rent was not 
impacted by your expressed interest to cohabitate. In addition, the amount of rent will not change 
due to your potential presence in the apartment. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 82028(a) defines a "gift" as any payment that confers a personal benefit on the 
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received. As a general rule, 
when a gift is received by the spouse of a public official and the official enjoys a direct benefit from 
the gift, the official is required to disclose the gift on his or her statement of economic interests. 


File No. A-18-251 
Page No. 3 
To reduce improper influence on public officials, one of the ways the Act regulates the 
receipt of gifts by public officials regards gifts to an official through a family member: 
Regulation 18943(c) provides that gifts to the benefit of your spouse will be considered gifts 
to you if either of the following applies: 
"(1) There is no established working, social, or similar relationship between the donor and 
the family member that would· suggest an association between the donor and the family member 
suitable or appropriate for making the type of payment provided to the family member." 
"(2) There is evidence to suggest the donor had a purpose to influence the official." 
To the contrary, your husband has a well-established working relationship with Raley's. 
Specifically, he has an almost five-year workirig relationship with the food retailer and was selected 
by Raley's from a pool of other management level employee applicants to attend the management 
training program. As such, he has an established "independent relationship" that lead to him 
receiving the offer to participate in the program. Additionally, there is no indication that Raley's 
intends to benefit you through the Food Industry Management program (See In re Cory (1975) 2 
FPPC Ops. 48.). In this case, it is rather unlikely that you will be involved in a decision specifically 
involving Raley's, and there is no evide_nce of any attempt by Raley's to influence you in your 
official capacity through the offer extended to your husband. Accordingly, the Act's gift 
prohibitions are inapplicable to the indirect benefits you may receive from the program. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
RPOC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
7ro~ 
By: 
Ryan P. O'Connor 
Counsel, Legal Division
